nodes.name: Initial Skin Trigger
nodes.description: Trauma, infection, stress, or drugs disrupt the skin barrier
nodes.body: Damaged keratinocytes release LL-37 antimicrobial peptide which complexes with self-DNA to activate pDCs via TLR7/TLR9. Examples: Koebner phenomenon, Streptococcal infection, Psychological stress, Beta-blockers, Lithium, NSAIDs.

nodes.name: LL-37
nodes.description: Cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide — the molecular spark of psoriasis
nodes.body: Released by damaged keratinocytes. Forms complexes with self-DNA. These complexes activate pDCs via TLR7/TLR9, converting a self-DNA signal (normally non-immunogenic) into a powerful immune activator. Produced by keratinocytes and neutrophils.

nodes.name: Plasmacytoid DC
nodes.description: First responder — produces type I interferons to activate mDCs
nodes.body: Activated by LL-37/DNA complexes via TLR7/TLR9. Produces IFN-alpha and IFN-beta which trigger mDC maturation. Markers: TLR7, TLR9, BDCA-2, CD123.

nodes.name: Myeloid DC
nodes.description: Command center — secretes TNF-alpha, IL-12, and IL-23
nodes.body: Matured by pDC interferons. Presents antigens and secretes TNF-alpha, IL-12, and IL-23. Significantly elevated in psoriatic lesions vs normal skin. Markers: CD11c, HLA-DR, CD80, CD86.

nodes.name: TNF-alpha
nodes.description: First validated biologic target — synergizes with IL-17 on keratinocytes
nodes.body: Directly activates keratinocytes, amplifies IL-23 release from DCs, and synergizes powerfully with IL-17. Promotes T-cell and neutrophil infiltration. Receptor: TNFR1/TNFR2. Pathway: NF-kB, MAPK. Molecular weight: 17.5 kDa.

nodes.name: IL-12
nodes.description: Drives Th1 differentiation — shares p40 subunit with IL-23
nodes.body: Drives naive T cells to Th1 differentiation. Shares p40 subunit with IL-23, hence targeted by ustekinumab. p35 mRNA largely unchanged in psoriatic vs normal skin. Subunits: p35, p40. Receptor: IL-12R-beta1/IL-12R-beta2. Pathway: JAK2/TYK2, STAT4. Molecular weight: 70 kDa.

nodes.name: IL-23
nodes.description: Master upstream regulator — drives and sustains Th17 differentiation
nodes.body: Master upstream regulator. Drives AND sustains Th17 differentiation. Elevated 10-100x in psoriatic vs normal skin. IL-23p19-specific biologics show the most durable clinical responses. Subunits: p19 (unique), p40 (shared with IL-12). Receptor: IL-23R/IL-12R-beta1. Pathway: JAK2/TYK2, STAT3. Molecular weight: 55 kDa.

nodes.name: Th1 Cells
nodes.description: Original candidate driver (pre-2005) — secondary to Th17 axis
nodes.body: Differentiated by IL-12 via JAK2/STAT4. Produce IFN-gamma and TNF-alpha. IFN-gamma arm partially independent of IL-23 blockade. Markers: CD4, T-bet, CXCR3.

nodes.name: Th17 Cells
nodes.description: Primary pathogenic effectors — driven by IL-23, produce IL-17
nodes.body: Primary pathogenic effectors. Driven and sustained by IL-23 via STAT3. Produce IL-17A, IL-17F, IL-21, and IL-22. GammaDelta T cells and CD8+ Tc17 cells also produce IL-17, creating multi-cellular amplification. Markers: CD4, ROR-gammat, CCR6, IL-23R.

nodes.name: IFN-gamma
nodes.description: From Th1 and NK cells — not fully suppressed by IL-23 blockade
nodes.body: Produced by Th1 cells and NK cells. Activates keratinocytes via JAK1/STAT1. Not fully suppressed by IL-23 blockade, suggesting a partially independent inflammatory arm contributing to non-response. Receptor: IFNGR1/IFNGR2. Pathway: JAK1/JAK2, STAT1. Molecular weight: 17 kDa.

nodes.name: IL-17A/F
nodes.description: Most directly pathogenic — IL-17A ~10-100x more potent than IL-17F
nodes.body: Most directly pathogenic cytokines in psoriasis. Bind IL-17RA/RC on keratinocytes activating NF-kB and MAPK. IL-17A ~10-100x more potent than IL-17F but IL-17F contributes substantially. Synergize with TNF-alpha. Isoforms: IL-17A homodimer, IL-17F homodimer, IL-17A/F heterodimer. Receptor: IL-17RA/IL-17RC. Pathway: NF-kB, MAPK.

nodes.name: IL-22
nodes.description: Drives keratinocyte hyperproliferation — compresses 28-day cycle to 3-5 days
nodes.body: Produced by Th17 and Th22 cells. Drives keratinocyte hyperproliferation and disrupts normal epidermal differentiation, compressing 28-day cycle to 3-5 days. Reduces barrier integrity. Receptor: IL-22R1/IL-10R2. Pathway: JAK1/TYK2, STAT3. Molecular weight: 24 kDa.

nodes.name: Keratinocytes
nodes.description: Central self-sustaining node — turnover 28d to 3-5d in psoriasis
nodes.body: Transform from passive structural cells to active immune amplifiers. Normal 28-day turnover compressed to 3-5 days. Produce CCL20 (recruits Th17), IL-23 (sustains loop), and amplification cytokines. Intracellular JAK/STAT and TYK2 pathways are drug targets. Activated by: IL-17A, IL-17F, TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma, IL-22. Produces: CCL20, IL-23, IL-6, IL-8, IL-36, LL-37, Beta-defensins, S100A8, S100A9.

nodes.name: CCL20
nodes.description: Key amplification chemokine — recruits Th17 via CCR6
nodes.body: Key chemokine in psoriasis amplification. Produced by keratinocytes in response to IL-17 stimulation. Binds exclusively to CCR6 receptor on Th17 cells, recruiting them back to the skin — closing the self-amplifying loop. Elevated in serum and lesions. Also known as MIP-3alpha, LARC. Receptor: CCR6.

nodes.name: NF-kB Pathway
nodes.description: Master transcription factor downstream of TNF-alpha and IL-17
nodes.body: Activated downstream of TNF-alpha, IL-17, and IL-1. Drives expression of inflammatory genes in keratinocytes including cytokines, chemokines, and antimicrobial peptides. TRAF6 is a key adapter linking IL-17RA signaling to NF-kB. Key components: IKK complex, IkB, p65/p50 dimer, TRAF6.

nodes.name: JAK/STAT Pathway
nodes.description: TYK2 links IL-23R to STAT3 — first oral targeted psoriasis therapy
nodes.body: Intracellular signaling cascade used by multiple psoriasis cytokines. TYK2 specifically links IL-23R and IL-12R signaling to STAT3/STAT4 activation. JAK1 links IFN-gamma and IL-22 receptors. TYK2 inhibition (deucravacitinib) is the first approved small molecule specifically targeting this pathway. Key nodes: TYK2 (activates STAT3/STAT4, downstream of IL-23R/IL-12R), JAK1 (activates STAT1/STAT3, downstream of IFN-gamma-R/IL-22R), JAK2 (activates STAT3/STAT4, downstream of IL-23R/IL-12R).

nodes.name: Adalimumab (Humira)
nodes.description: Anti-TNF-alpha monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Fully human anti-TNF-alpha monoclonal antibody. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid (weeks). Durability: moderate. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis. Targets TNF-alpha directly, blocking its binding to TNFR1 and TNFR2.

nodes.name: Etanercept (Enbrel)
nodes.description: TNF receptor-IgG fusion protein
nodes.body: TNF receptor-IgG fusion protein that acts as a decoy receptor for TNF-alpha. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid. Durability: moderate. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis. Unique mechanism: soluble receptor fusion rather than monoclonal antibody.

nodes.name: Infliximab (Remicade)
nodes.description: Chimeric anti-TNF-alpha monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Chimeric (mouse/human) anti-TNF-alpha monoclonal antibody. Route: intravenous infusion. Onset: rapid. Durability: moderate. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis. IV administration allows high peak serum levels.

nodes.name: Certolizumab Pegol (Cimzia)
nodes.description: PEGylated anti-TNF-alpha Fab fragment
nodes.body: PEGylated humanized anti-TNF-alpha Fab fragment. No Fc region; does not cross placenta — preferred in pregnancy. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid. Durability: moderate. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.

nodes.name: Ustekinumab (Stelara)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-12/23 p40 monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Fully human monoclonal antibody targeting the shared p40 subunit of IL-12 and IL-23. Blocks both IL-12 (Th1) and IL-23 (Th17) pathways simultaneously. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: moderate. Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, and Crohn's disease.

nodes.name: Risankizumab (Skyrizi)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-23p19 monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting the p19 subunit specific to IL-23. Does not block IL-12. Dosing every 12 weeks after induction; high long-term clearance rates. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: moderate. Durability: very high. Approved for plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, and Crohn's disease.

nodes.name: Guselkumab (Tremfya)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-23p19 monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Fully human monoclonal antibody targeting the p19 subunit specific to IL-23. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: moderate. Durability: very high. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.

nodes.name: Tildrakizumab (Ilumetri)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-23p19 monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting the p19 subunit of IL-23. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: moderate. Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis.

nodes.name: Secukinumab (Cosentyx)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Fully human monoclonal antibody that selectively binds and neutralizes IL-17A. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid (2-4 weeks). Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.

nodes.name: Ixekizumab (Taltz)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Humanized monoclonal antibody selectively targeting IL-17A. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid. Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.

nodes.name: Bimekizumab (Bimzelx)
nodes.description: Dual anti-IL-17A and IL-17F monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Humanized monoclonal antibody that neutralizes both IL-17A and IL-17F (dual blockade). BE RADIANT trial: superior skin clearance vs secukinumab at 16 weeks; increased Candida infection risk vs IL-17A alone. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid. Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, and ankylosing spondylitis.

nodes.name: Brodalumab (Kyntheum)
nodes.description: Anti-IL-17RA receptor monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Fully human monoclonal antibody targeting the IL-17RA receptor. Blocks all IL-17 isoforms: IL-17A, IL-17F, IL-17A/F, IL-17C, IL-17E via receptor blockade. Black box warning for suicidal ideation. Route: subcutaneous injection. Onset: rapid. Durability: high. Approved for plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.

nodes.name: Deucravacitinib (Sotyktu)
nodes.description: First-in-class selective oral TYK2 inhibitor (allosteric)
nodes.body: First-in-class oral selective TYK2 inhibitor. Allosteric mechanism — binds the pseudokinase regulatory domain of TYK2, avoiding JAK1/JAK2/JAK3 off-target effects. No required lab monitoring. Route: oral daily tablet. Onset: moderate. Durability: high. Approved for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis. Disrupts IL-23R and IL-12R signaling downstream of TYK2 to STAT3/STAT4.
